title: PurgeRequest
description: A request to invalidate or evict one or more entries from a cache or CDN edge, identified by exact keys, URL patterns, or surrogate tags.

purge_id.description: Identifier assigned by the cache provider to track the purge request and its propagation status.

scope.description: How the purge targets are interpreted by the cache.

targets.description: List of keys, URLs, surrogate keys, or patterns to purge.

mode.description: Purge mode. Soft marks entries stale-while-revalidate; hard evicts immediately; instant requests global propagation; revalidate triggers a background fetch.

cache_id.description: Identifier of the cache instance, zone, service, or distribution the purge applies to.

namespace.description: Optional logical namespace, database number, or KV bucket scoping the purge.

completed_at.description: Timestamp the purge completed globally across all cache nodes or edge POPs.

status.description: Current state of the purge request.

submitted_by.description: Identity, API key label, or principal that submitted the purge.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the purge, often recorded for audit.
